rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GARY Y. TAKEOKA
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and consider whether to take this petition off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/27/2025): 1) Re the DE-120 form (notice of hearing) filed on 06/23/2025: i) Item 1 must specify petitioner's capacity as "executor". ii) Direct notice must be given to each minor beneficiary of the devisee trust. See CRC 7.51(d). iii) Separate notice must be given to each person having legal custody of and residing with each minor of the devisee trust. See CRC 7.51(d). A verified allegation must be made to identify each person having legal custody of and residing with each minor. iv) Solomon Fong and Nolan Yamada appear to be adults now. Direct notice must be given to each. v) Lowell High School's address is different from its address listed on the case-initiating petition for probate. A verified allegation must be made to confirm the current address. 2) An allegation must be made as to whether there are any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution. See LRSF 14.35(J)(1). 3) Has petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 4) Please provide proof of compliance of Probate Code section 10533(a)(1) or (2) showing that the estate cash on hand is fully insured or is otherwise held in the form of authorized investment(s). 5) The decedent died more than 2 years ago, but no first interim account has been filed. See LRSF 14.34(A) limiting the first account period to 2 years from the date of death; see also section 10950(a) re the Court's authority to order the first account on its own motion. If petitioner in her capacity as trustee of the devisee trust has waived the first account, please file a signed waiver of account. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HOWARD PARKER JOHNSTON, JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/22/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A "second amended petition" was e-filed on 11/26/25, prepared on pleading paper. A petition for probate is a mandatory Judicial Council form. If the pleading was intended to be an amendment to the amended petition filed on 11/6/25, the "second amended petition" should be withdrawn and a proper amendment (prepared on pleading paper and captioned "Amendment to Amended Petition," etc.) should be filed [Please see CRC 7.3 for definitions.) (2) Proof of publication must clearly indicate the full title of the pleading to be heard: "Second Amended Petition for Letters of Administration," etc., not "Petition for Probate and Amendments." No proof of publication of notice of the amended petition filed on 11/6/25 has been filed yet. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) Whether or not the maternal heirs would be forthcoming if they knew of any paternal heirs, it is not their duty to know who the paternal heirs are. How did Petitioner determine that Decedent's paternal uncles Robert G. Johnston and James Barclay Johnston, Jr., had no known children? (5) The heirship chart attached to the "declaration" filed on 12/5/25 is confusing; the horizontal line connecting Decedent to his maternal cousins (all shown as a generation below Decedent) makes no sense, and several cousins appear to be descended from each other instead of being of the same generation, as the verbal descriptions indicate. The child of a first cousin is a first cousin once removed, not a second cousin. The child of a deceased first cousin once removed is a first cousin twice removed, not a third cousin. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 4/8/26. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PASQUALINA ANGELINA BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/22/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice given to the heirs has been filed. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Exhibit A attached to the supplement filed on 12/15/25 does not indicate who generated the information. Usually an heirship determination is accompanied by a cover letter or verification by the heir finder company. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/8/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARIEL JOEL LAZIER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/8/26. (No earlier available date.) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. The waivers of bond are incomplete (not dated). A copy of the power of attorney empowering Nancy Lazier as agent must be filed. Notwithstanding waivers of bond by the heirs, the petitioner must set forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] (Issues must be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to the petition. [CRC 7.3.]) Courtesy copies of any responsive papers must be received by the assigned examiner by 3/25/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
